attacking_style: Pure sprinter who finishes from reduced bunches and uphill finales rather than flat mass sprints. Thrives in selective stages where climbing or terrain shed pure fastmen before final 1-3km.
team_tactics: Consistently undermanned leadout support - frequently races with single leadout (WRIGHT) or no organized train. Relies on individual positioning and raw speed rather than textbook sprint trains seen with rivals.
terrain_preference: Reduced sprints after climbs or coastal splits; handles uphill finales and selective stages exceptionally. Survives climbs that shed pure sprinters. Excels when bunch reduced to 30-50 riders. Less dominant in pure flat mass sprints.
vulnerability: Exposed when isolated without leadout support - loses intermediate sprint classifications to denial tactics. Full-strength opposition sprint trains (VAN POPPEL example) exploit his lack of organized leadout. Sprint timing inconsistencies when well-positioned but unable to convert.
key_patterns: Won both Grand Tour stages from reduced sprints after selective terrain (2022 Giro examples).
Repeated top-5 finishes despite mechanical/positioning setbacks show exceptional recovery ability (Tirreno puncture, multiple Giro stages).
Consistently competitive without leadout train across 6+ races; raw speed compensates for tactical deficit.
Thrives as underdog in selective finales; beat VAN DER POEL twice in reduced sprints.
Climbing resilience allows survival when others shed; stayed competitive through Giro's lumpy stages.
Bonus second hunting behavior (Tour de Suisse intermediate sprint) shows tactical maturity development.
form_trajectory: positive: June 2023 Tour de Suisse win confirmed full recovery from February crash. Upward trajectory from 2022 Giro breakthrough through 2023 classics podiums demonstrates consistent year-over-year progression.
